title: Mechanical liver dissociation

steps: Add 55 mL of FCS to a bottle of 500 mL of RPMI1640 with L-Glutamine.
Prepare GentleMACS C-tubes by filling them with 9 mL RPMI 1640 medium + 10% FCS and store the tubes at 4 °C .
Fill the syringe with 20 mL PBS and connect a 23G needle to the syringe.
IMPORTANT! Proceed with liver perfusion shortly after euthanasia to prevent clotting of the blood. Euthanize the mice by decapitation.
Position mouse in dorsal recumbency and pin down. Spray the abdominal and thoracic region with 70% EtOH.
Make a parallel incision from the caudal to cranial side starting from the mouse’s abdomen until the thoracic region.
Gently move the intestines to the right side to get access to the liver using forceps and carefully flip the median lobe and left lobe to the cranial side to visualize the portal vein.
Insert the tip of the needle in the hepatic portal vein. Slowly push 20 mL PBS through. The liver will become tense and pale if the perfusion is correctly performed.
Carefully dissect the perfused liver and transfer into the C-tube containing the medium. Remove gallbladder during dissection of the mouse liver.
Tightly close C-tube and attach it upside down onto the GentleMACS Octo Dissociator.
After termination of the program, detach C-tube from the GentleMACS Octo Dissociator and keep the tubes on ice until further processing.
Dispose the needle in an approved sharps container.
creator: Wim Pierson
